rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL CORRAL JR ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For First Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus & Court Reporter Fee Paid
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2021 line 1. PETITIONERS MIGUEL CORRAL, DEANNA MICROS, SUELEN VIERA VALE, PHILIP CLARKE, RONAN MOHAN, KATE MORHAM, KYLE MORHAM and SHERYL SAMSON'S Notice Of Hearing For First Amended Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus is DENIED. Moving party failed to shat that the ALJ/agency improperly applied "reasonable reliance" standard existing at the time of the ALJ's decision or that based on the evidence before the ALJ no reasonable person could reach the conclusion that the real party in interest reasonably relied on the pass through provisions. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ERNESTO E. ALONSO VS. JUAN GONZALEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Marlon Austria, Drm Holdings, Inc. Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANTS MARLON AUSTRIA and DRM HOLDINGS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a viable cause of action against buyer's agent/broker. Plaintiff must allege facts (not conclusions) in good faith to support each element of each cause of action including but not limited to facts re: brokers/agents' knowledge of the foundation and garage door defects or obligation to discover such defects.=(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PONTE GADEA CALIFORNIA, LLC VS. OLD NAVY, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Documents And Provide Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT OLD NAVY, LLC's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Documents And Provide Further Discovery Responses is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: (1) response to interrogatory number 4 must be provided in yes/no format at this time and for this set of discovery; (2) response to request #7, shall be limited to marketing materials that were given to the general public and not to any particular prospective retail tenant (3) no responses to request Requests #8-10 and 16 shall be provided at this time/no notice to third parties; (4) response to Request #12 shall be provided as to periodic reports related to Old Navy and shall not be provided otherwise/no notice to third parties; (5) responses to requests 28-30 shall be provided accompanied by a privilege log if privilege is asserted; (6) responses to requests 31 and 32 shall be provided; (7) response to request 36 shall not be provided; (8) response to request 39 shall be provided as it relates to communications with Old Navy, otherwise denied/no notice to third parties. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: INSUN SON ET AL VS. DORIAN PEECHER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 17, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTS PS FUNDING INC., FCI LENDER SERVICES, INC and PEER STREET, INC's. DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of contract and violation of CC 1632 for Plaintiffs to allege in good faith facts in support of each element of these causes of action and to attach contract (or quite them verbatim in the complaint). Demurrer is otherwise sustained without leave to amend, non-opposition filed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
